former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Sōsha Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|qid=Q2921|1=October 10}}{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Miyake Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kou-cho, Suzuka) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Miyake-jinja (Kou-cho, Suzuka) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Honden}}]] '''Miyake Shrine''' (みやけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kō-chō, {{ill|lt=Suzuka|Q207311|qid=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|qid=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, is presumed to be the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. At the end of the Meiji period it merged '''E Shrine''' (えじんじゃ, former village shrine), another candidate shrine for a shikinaisha. === Enshrined deities === Current enshrined deities are the following three[http://www.amigo.ne.jp/~chitonr2/jinjaichiran_suzuka.html List of shrines in Mie Prefecture > Suzuka City] (Mie Prefecture Shrine Office).. * {{ill|lt=Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} - deity of the merged E Shrine. In Meiji shrine registers, the deities are listed as Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto, Ōanamuchi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. It is also said that after Meiji-end mergers of village shrines in Kō-chō, 24 deities are now enshrined{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. === History === The founding is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' completed in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' records “Miyake Shrine” in Suzuka District, {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, and this shrine is regarded as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for that shikinaisha{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Miyake Shrine (Miyake-chō, Suzuka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyake Shrine}} in Miyake-chō, Suzuka{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. “Miyake” is thought to derive from ancient court-controlled lands called {{ill|lt=miyake (屯倉)|Q11465761|qid=Q11465761|1=Miyake}}; from that viewpoint, Miyake-chō is often considered more plausible as the location of Suzuka’s miyake and the shikinaisha “Miyake Shrine”{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. This shrine, by contrast, was commonly called “Sōsha Daimyōjin” until the Edo period, and appears to have been renamed “Miyake Shrine” only after Meiji; no old example of this site being called “Miyake Shrine” is known{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. Regarding this shikinaisha “Miyake Shrine,” a possible relation has also been pointed out to “Kokushi no kami Miyake no Muraji Iwatoko” in the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', entry for June 24, first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|qid=Q30601|1=672}}), when Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu) reached Ise Suzuka{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Another theory identifies this shrine instead with “Ōshika Miyake Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Kawawa District|Q11553861|qid=Q11553861|1=Kawawa district}}{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. There is also a known theory placing this shrine as the sōsha of Ise Province{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. A sōsha is a shrine near a provincial capital where provincial deities were jointly enshrined, appearing nationwide from around the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. For Ise Province, the mid-{{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}} provincial office site has been identified as the Chōjayashiki ruins (national historic site “Ise Kokufu ruins”) away from Miyake Shrine, but the later Nara to Heian provincial office site remains uncertain, with one theory placing it around present Kō-chō where this shrine stands“Ise Kokufu,” ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 24: Place Names of Mie Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1983.. Around Kō-chō, remains related to the provincial capital have been found at Miyake Shrine ruins and Tennōyama-nishi ruins, but the core kokuga site has not been discovered{{PDFlink|[http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/kokufu-p.pdf "National Historic Site Ise Kokufu Ruins - Excavation of Chōjayashiki Site, Suzuka, Mie"]}} (Suzuka Archaeological Museum).[http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/site100/0495.htm Miyake Shrine Ruins] (Suzuka Archaeological Museum website).. If the shrine name “Miyake” is assumed to be old in origin (not created in Meiji), another theory derives this “Miyake” from the Ise provincial capital granary{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. It had been commonly called “Sōsha Daimyōjin” through the Edo period, and was renamed “Miyake Shrine” then{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. In March {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|qid=Q2056|1=1908}} and May {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|qid=Q2076|1=1911}}, shrines from villages in Kō-chō were merged{{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. Among merged shrines was E Shrine, itself a candidate for the Suzuka District shikinaisha “E Shrine” (another candidate exists in Shimo-no-shō-chō, Kameyama){{Sfn|Miyake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Igo Masaaki|authorlink=井後政晏|chapter=Miyake Shrine|year=1977|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 7|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Miyake Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1977}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Miyake Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 24 Place Names of Mie Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490244|ref={{Harvid|Miyake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Basic Study on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Ichinomiya system|2000}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miyake Shrine (Q22120521)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q22120521)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miyake Shrine (Q22120521)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:History of Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Province]]
